meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world SaaS project as a student developer.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering. By combining the project-scoping power of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you can transform a vague concept into a functional MVP. This workflow automates the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on logic, architecture, and shipping your first portfolio-ready application.
content.intro:
The screen glows, a blinking cursor mocks your silence. You have the skills, the syntax, and the drive, yet the 'no project ideas' paradox keeps you paralyzed. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a system to bridge the gap between abstract desire and concrete code.

This isn't just another tutorial; it is an architectural blueprint for the modern AI-native developer. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will stop chasing ideas and start building assets. Let’s turn your blank editor into a production-grade codebase.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a high-impact, achievable project scope tailored to your current skill level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior SaaS architect. Generate a project scope for a beginner developer that solves a real-world problem, includes a clear database schema, and ensures a 48-hour MVP timeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Initialize a project skeleton based on the Antigravity scope: [PASTE_SCOPE_HERE]'

steps.title: Establish the Technical Stack
steps.description: Set up your environment with Claude Code to ensure all dependencies are clean and conflicts are resolved immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Recommend a modern, lightweight tech stack (e.g., Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind) that prioritizes speed and developer experience for a first-time build.

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Generate the folder structure and configuration files to prevent 'configuration fatigue' before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project structure for an MVP. Include folders for components, hooks, services, and utils.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold the directory structure based on the architectural plan'

steps.title: Implement Feature Iteration
steps.description: Build the MVP feature-by-feature using Claude Code's contextual awareness to keep your code DRY and modular.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a step-by-step feature breakdown for user authentication and dashboard view, focusing on best security practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement authentication using Supabase Auth with a protected dashboard route'

steps.title: Refactor and Document
steps.description: Use AI to audit your code for readability and generate technical documentation for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase for performance bottlenecks and suggest improvements for clean, maintainable code.

pro_tips: Always use the '--fix' flag in Claude Code to let the model self-correct linting errors before you even see them.
Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'system instructions' for your project; keep them in a README.md file to maintain consistency.
When stuck, ask Claude Code to 'explain the trade-offs' of a specific architectural choice to deepen your engineering knowledge.
Break your features into micro-tasks; Claude Code performs significantly better with 10 small prompts than one monolithic request.
Leverage the 'checkpoint' feature in your version control immediately after a Claude Code session to protect your progress.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, document the build process in a blog post, push the repository to GitHub, and share it in the community discord for peer review.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build anything. Don't let the 'no project ideas' trap hold you back—your first MVP is waiting to be coded. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
